steps.title: Read the singular complement
steps.content: The sentence says the entities share one feature: being "an island or a group of islands." Because the complement is singular, the wording should describe each entity individually, not the entities as one collective group.

steps.title: Reject agreement problems
steps.content: Choice B pairs plural all with singular is. Choice D pairs singular each with plural are. Choice A uses all with are, but that collective phrasing does not fit the singular complement that follows.

steps.title: Use the distributive subject
steps.content: Each of them is treats every geopolitical entity one at a time and correctly pairs the singular subject each with the singular verb is. The answer is C.
